description: The one classification of every .gaia/local entry, transcribed by hand from the frozen state-model classification (never generated from code, which is what is under correction). Consumers read this instead of hand-maintaining their own lists. `path` is relative to .gaia/local/. A `path` may join more than one concrete on-disk shape with '|' when a single logical entry covers several filenames (e.g. a clearance marker's .ok/.refused/.dispositions.json siblings); each '|'-joined alternative is matched independently under the entry's own `match` rule. A `path` may also carry a '<placeholder>' token for a variable path segment (a spec id, a digest, a worktree name); for `match: glob` a placeholder behaves as a shell '*' wildcard, and for `match: prefix` only the literal text before the first '<' is significant. `residue` lists known dead-feature/legacy paths that are real on some machines but are not live state: reported if found, never treated as drift and never a live entry.

entries.keyed_by: singleton per-clone value; correct via idempotent overwrite, no partition key needed
entries.why: Per-clone /gaia-init and /setup-gaia step-completion record. A per-tree copy would re-run setup steps per worktree, which is wrong for a fact about the clone, not the tree.

entries.keyed_by: singleton machine-scoped caches, each overwritten in place; update-gaia/<tag>/ additionally partitions by release tag
entries.why: Machine-scoped release/update-check and statusline-guard state (update-check.json, update-gaia/<tag>/, serena-guard/) that is a property of the machine, not of any one tree.

entries.keyed_by: content digest over the files the audited member owns (per member)
entries.why: A clearance marker attests a Code Audit Team member reviewed content; in git the tree is the content, so the key is the content, and the symlinked drop zone lets one gate judge every checkout's markers.

entries.keyed_by: base-sha plus branch (target; base-sha alone collides across worktrees cut from one main tip)
entries.why: Co-written into the symlinked audit store; the branch discriminator is the missing partition that keeps two trees' findings from overwriting each other.
A CI-observability breadcrumb written by the frontend audit and read by the CI print step, keyed by the tree sha and never re-keyed as a clearance.
Redacted local security detail, keyed by the commit it describes, in the shared audit store.

entries.keyed_by: singleton store; a cross-tree file lock plus self-identifying rows (session_id + spec/plan id + seq)
entries.why: An append-only cost ledger every tree contributes to; correctness comes from the lock, not from partitioning.

entries.keyed_by: singleton; idempotent overwrite of a cache whose authority is a repo-global GitHub fact
entries.why: A worktree debt fix must arm main's count cache, not a discarded per-tree copy; the authority is the GitHub tech-debt label, not this file.
A RED observation is a claim about one working tree at one commit; sharing it across trees would be a category error. The identity rule's own reference example of correctly per-tree state.

A worthiness observation is a claim about one working tree's emergent-surface tests at one commit, the same kind of per-tree fact as a RED observation; reopened per-tree to match its RED-ledger sibling. Its filing under the symlinked audit/ directory is a keys-phase relocation, not its target scope.
A human-facing local forensics report read in the checkout that produced it; no cross-tree consumer. Named in the write-guard's own non-exempt list.
The one live handoff for this tree's session; a handoff written in one tree and read from another is exactly the wrong-tree confusion the checkout-boundary guard exists to catch.

An operator's declines on this checkout's hardening candidates. Forks with the worktree today, a defect since a decline dies with the tree; classified at its per-tree target with the persistence fix deferred to a later phase.
The SPEC-number allocation's authority is the remote immutable spec/NNN tag; the ledger is main's cache and the plan reference contracts the SPEC-NNN folders to the main checkout.
The plan folder stays in the main checkout by contract; worktree-mode orchestrators write PROGRESS.md back to main.
A PR-capture written under main's cache for the merge flow, main-anchored not per-tree; currently unkeyed last-writer-wins, with a per-branch key as the keys-phase target.
A spec-to-plan chain guard sentinel deliberately resolved to main, keyed by session id, so it holds across a tree switch within one session.
Transient creation-coordination locks that must live in one place, because they serialize attempts made before either candidate worktree exists; hold no durable data, keyed by worktree name.

One identity per clone, used in ping/telemetry. Forks per worktree path today via a hash of the repo root path, a defect; classified at its main-only target scope with the fix deferred to a later phase.
An update-preference file written only from main in the shipped workflow, because the /update-deps flow refuses to run from a linked worktree before any write happens.
Read-anchored to main (the statusline command resolves to main) or operator-authored per machine; single copies with no per-tree meaning. Hand-authored, not GAIA-generated.
Per-machine markers (a dismissed nudge, a sandbox-resolution result, a short-lived setup sentinel); one value per clone.

This program's own pre-allowlisted artifact for a future update-driven migration; no shipped writer exists yet. Its eventual scope is a forward inference (main-anchored, matching every other update-flow cache, since /update-gaia and /update-deps both refuse to run from a worktree) rather than a fact about shipped code.
The live in-flight SPEC draft, its pre-dispatch retry snapshots, and the gate-1 drift-detection snapshot for one authoring session; keyed by spec id, deleted at canonical save or on discard.
Per-tree session shape (start time, Socratic question-ceiling counter) for one SPEC-authoring session; the holder's own working state, keyed by spec id, stays where it is written.
The session-host liveness lock for one SPEC-authoring session; deliberately resolved to main so the cross-tree mutex is reachable from both trees, keyed by spec id.
Self-review and adversarial-audit scratch (per-lens findings, refutation verdicts, coverage log) for one in-progress SPEC-authoring session; keyed by spec id, deleted at canonical save or discard.
A cost-ledger breadcrumb recording whether an adversarial audit ran this SPEC or plan session; deliberately resolved to main, keyed by spec/plan id, read-then-deleted by the cost tally.
A defer-flag cache for SPEC/plan wiki promotion, and a per-UAT content-hash mirror for the UAT-write idempotency check; both keyed by SPEC or plan id.
gaia-react-perf measurement run output, keyed by run id.
A daily-cached spec-kit version check with no partition key; harmless as an idempotent-overwrite cache since each tree's concurrent write simply re-refreshes the same daily answer.
/gaia-audit findings reports and transient gh-issue-body staging files, keyed by timestamp/run stamp, self-pruned by their own producer in the shared audit directory.

/health-audit per-run bucket-executor output and its Comprehensive-phase sibling, keyed by run stamp, self-pruned to a bounded number of recent runs by the health-audit runbook, in the shared audit directory.

residue.why: Opt-in config for the removed mentorship feature. Not on the janitor's outlier allowlist, so a stray one is also eventually reaped by the outlier sweep; actively deleted by the mentorship-residue cleanup sweep.
Removed mentorship feature's cloud event stream, deleted by the mentorship-residue cleanup sweep.
Removed mentorship feature's analytics reports, deleted by the mentorship-residue cleanup sweep.
Orphaned marker from the removed mentorship feature; both its writer and reader are gone. Deleted by the mentorship-residue cleanup sweep.
Residue pattern from a deleted carry-forward feature; no current writer anywhere in the codebase, reaped unconditionally by the outlier sweep if one is ever found.
Per-checkout sentinel recording that the mentorship-residue cleanup sweep already ran here; the sweep it belongs to is itself transitional cleanup for a removed feature.
Legacy pre-existing archived-plan layout; the live plan-archive path reduces a plan folder in place today and does not move it here. Read only by repair and migration tooling.
Legacy pre-existing archived-SPEC layout; the live spec-archive path reduces a SPEC folder in place today and does not move it here. Read only by repair and migration tooling.

rm_whitelist.why: Archived plan folders are regenerable scratch an agent may clear; the plans/ ledger itself is main-anchored state that is never bulk-deleted, so only its children are whitelisted.
Individual spec-scratch folders are clearable, but specs/ is the main-anchored SPEC store and is never bulk-deleted; children only.
Audit markers and reports are self-pruning scratch; the shared audit/ store is symlinked structure that is never bulk-deleted; children only.
A per-tree handoff file is disposable session scratch; the handoff/ container is kept, only its contents are cleared.
Cache entries are regenerable machine/session scratch; the cache/ container is kept, only its contents are cleared.
Build output regenerated by the build; safe to remove wholesale (rm -rf dist), so the directory itself and its children are both whitelisted. Repo-root-relative, outside .gaia/local.
Build output regenerated by the build; safe to remove wholesale, directory and children alike. Repo-root-relative, outside .gaia/local.

drop_zones.why: The Code Audit Team clearance-marker store, symlinked into every worktree; tooling drops markers here.
Health-audit's archived per-run buckets; a persistent archive destination kept even when momentarily empty.
The machine- and session-cache container many flows write into.
Legacy archived-plan destination, preserved for repair and migration tooling.
Legacy archived-SPEC destination, preserved for repair and migration tooling.

integrity_snapshot.why: Durable, leaf-authored SPEC folders; a destructive script deleting one (the SPEC-031 incident) must be caught, never mistaken for background churn.
Durable, leaf-authored plan folders and their PROGRESS ledgers; irreplaceable working state a run must not silently alter.
The live per-tree session handoff; leaf-authored durable content, unlike the background-churned debt/telemetry/audit/cache/red-ledger dirs the snapshot deliberately excludes.
